args.task:
Perform comprehensive black-box reconnaissance and attack-surface mapping for the target domain: http://scaa.us

Scope:
- Passive and active subdomain enumeration (subfinder, DNS bruteforce, certificate transparency)
- Port scanning and service/version detection (naabu, nmap) across TCP ports (1-65535) and common UDP services as applicable
- HTTP(S) probing and fingerprinting (httpx) for all discovered hosts
- Web crawling and JavaScript analysis (katana/httpx) to enumerate endpoints, API routes, parameters, and hidden paths
- Directory and file discovery (ffuf-like techniques) including robots.txt, sitemap.xml, backup files, common admin paths
- WAF detection and technology fingerprinting
- Capture proxy traffic and produce sitemap entries with request IDs for follow-up testing

Operational constraints:
- Apply reasonable rate limits to avoid DoS
- Treat testing as fully authorized; proceed without user prompts
- Record all findings with artifacts (nmap/naabu outputs, httpx probes, discovered endpoints, JS-extracted API calls)

Deliverables:
- Target map: discovered hostnames, resolved IPs, open ports, services, and versions
- Web endpoint inventory: URL, method, parameters, and input points (forms, JSON APIs, query params)
- Sitemap and prioritized list of high-value targets for focused vulnerability agents (SQLi, XSS, SSRF, IDOR, Auth/JWT, File upload)
- Proxy capture scope IDs / request IDs for key interactions to enable repeat_request-based validation
- Notable artifacts: robots.txt, sitemap.xml, exposed config files, interesting JS files and extracted endpoints, WAF/stack fingerprinting results
- Recommended next-step subagents and priority order for targeted vulnerability testing
